findings.issue: acceptInvitation() in api.ts:44 uses raw fetch without AbortSignal or timeout support. InvitationCompletePage.tsx:66 calls it without passing the effect's abortController.signal, breaking the cancellation chain established in Round 13 for redeemOAuth. If the user navigates away during acceptInvitation, the POST completes and the invitation is consumed irreversibly, even though the guard at line 68 discards the UI result. The function also lacks the 30s timeout that makeRequest provides.
findings.remediation: Add optional signal parameter to acceptInvitation(), pass it to fetch(), and thread abortController.signal from InvitationCompletePage.tsx:66. Alternatively, route acceptInvitation through makeRequest to gain both signal and timeout support.

findings.issue: getInvitation() in api.ts:36 uses raw fetch without AbortSignal or timeout. InvitationPage.tsx:27 calls it in a useEffect without cancellation support. The active flag prevents stale setState but the HTTP request runs to completion after unmount, and a slow/stalled server will hang indefinitely with no timeout.
findings.remediation: Add optional signal parameter to getInvitation() and pass an AbortController signal from the useEffect, with cleanup abort on unmount.

findings.issue: oauthApi.init() in oauth.ts:18 does not accept an AbortSignal parameter, unlike oauthApi.redeem() which does. Both LoginPage (AppRouter.tsx:51) and InvitationPage (InvitationPage.tsx:51) call initOAuth without cancellation support. If the init request stalls, there is no way to cancel it.
findings.remediation: Add optional signal parameter to oauthApi.init() and thread it through to makeRequest(), matching the pattern already used by oauthApi.redeem().

findings.issue: No tests exist for anySignal() or makeRequest() in utils.ts. The signal forwarding contract for oauthApi.redeem is untested — oauth.test.ts:73 calls redeem without a signal, so the signal-to-fetch plumbing has no regression coverage.
findings.remediation: Add tests verifying that (a) makeRequest forwards options.signal to fetch via anySignal, (b) anySignal propagates abort from any input signal, and (c) the timeout controller fires after REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS.
